title: HTTP Semantics, Roles, Representations, and Layered Evidence · Go Client Systems · Program Defense
summary: A release board evaluating a changed infrastructure-provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for http semantics, roles, representations, and layered evidence, producing protocol evidence map. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go 1.26 net/http protocol evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Distinguish client, server, user agent, origin, intermediary, gateway, proxy, and cache roles per exchange. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Separate a resource from the selected representation and its metadata, content, and current validity. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Trace request construction, routing, transport, intermediary handling, response interpretation, and cleanup as distinct stages. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
